prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for benzaldehyde were computed with the chemkit `fukui-reactivity` skill using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier `standard`) in PySCF, in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1.  The calculation was a single-point analysis on the user-supplied geometry file `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/benzaldehyde.xyz` (no re-optimization; thus DFT//input-XYZ).  Density fitting was **not** used; no implicit solvation model was requested, so this is a gas-phase result.  Both N, N+1, and N–1 states converged (integrity.status = "ok", trustworthy = true).  The live PySCF output was streamed to the standard chemkit `.out` log file announced at run start.

Key results (Mulliken-based indices; atoms are 1-indexed in the original XYZ order):
• Most electrophilic site: atom 3 (C, carbonyl carbon) with f⁺ = 0.39938967.
• Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (O, carbonyl oxygen) with f⁻ = 0.23839906.

2 C  –0.3517 / –0.0071 / –0.3446

14 H –0.1817 / 0.1258 / –0.3076

Method provenance
Property: Fukui functions f⁺, f⁻, dual descriptor (Mulliken partition)
Level of theory: B3LYP/def2-TZVP, no density fitting (PySCF 2.x via chemkit)
Geometry: user-supplied benzaldehyde.xyz (single-point; not re-optimized)
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (N state); cation/anions: +1/–1, doublet
SCF convergence: achieved for N, N+1, N–1 states; sum(f⁺)=1.0000, sum(f⁻)=1.0000
Warnings: see below

• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present

Integrity verdict: trustworthy = true
